qids.subject: Computer Organization & Architecture
qids.prose: Following table indicates the latencies of operations between the instruction producing the result and instruction using the result. Consider the following code segment: Load R1, Loc 1; Load R1 from memory location Loc1 Load R2, Loc 2; Load R2 from memory location Loc 2 Add R1, R2, R1; Add R1 and R2

qids.subject: Computer Organization & Architecture
qids.prose: Assume that EA = (X)+ is the effective address equal to the contents of location X, with X incremented by one word length after the effective address is calculated; EA = -(X) is the effective address equal to the contents of location X, with X decremented by one word length before the effective addr

qids.subject: Computer Organization & Architecture
qids.prose: In interleaved memory organization, consecutive words are stored in consecutive memory modules in _______ interleaving, whereas consecutive words are stored within the module in ________ interleaving.

qids.subject: Computer Organization & Architecture
qids.prose: A CPU has a cache with block size bytes. The main memory has banks, each bank being bytes wide. Consecutive - byte chunks are mapped on consecutive banks with wrap-around.
